content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

I found 2 correctness issues in the new bead-export logic, both centered on explicit-id reuse and same-title matching in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`. Targeted bead-export tests passed, but these edge cases are not covered by the new test set.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Short explicit `bead_id` values can bind to the wrong existing bead. `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` probes the raw short id before the milestone-qualified form and accepts the first successful `br show` result without checking that it belongs to the current milestone or matches the canonicalized ref (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2256-2261, 2276-2282`). Because `BeadProposal.bead_id` is documented as a stable id or suffix, a plan entry like `bead-2` should resolve within the current milestone; today it can instead reuse an unrelated bead that happens to share that short id, and the export will then attach parent-child links and planning comments to the wrong issue. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] remediation_hint: Prefer the milestone-qualified candidate first, or validate that any raw-id hit resolves back to the current milestone/proposal before reusing it.
   Rationale: This is a direct correctness problem in the new export path: one explicit-id lookup can mutate an unrelated bead and corrupt the milestone graph. The vulnerable path is in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2256-2261, 2276-2282`.
2. finding_summary: Explicit-id matches are not removed from the same-title reuse cache, so two distinct proposals can collapse onto one existing bead during a single export. `materialize_bead_entry()` returns immediately when `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` succeeds (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1796-1800, 2247-2273`), which leaves that bead's summary in `existing_by_title`. Later, `find_existing_materialized_bead()` can reuse the same summary for another proposal with the same title because `identity_label_matches()` treats beads that have no materialized identity label as matching any proposal/workstream label (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1936-1950, 2127-2219`). A legacy or manually labeled explicit bead that only carries `milestone:*` can therefore satisfy two plan nodes, collapsing `planned_bead_id_map` and dependency wiring onto one actual bead. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] remediation_hint: When an explicit-id lookup succeeds, either remove that bead from `existing_by_title` immediately or tighten same-title fallback so unlabeled legacy beads cannot be reused for a second distinct proposal in the same run.
   Rationale: This is another export-time correctness gap: two separate bundle proposals can be materialized as the same live bead, which breaks dependency resolution and the exported id map. The relevant control flow is in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1796-1800, 1936-1950, 2127-2219, 2247-2273`.
